title: Rocket Lab Announces Third Quarter 2025 Financial Results, Posts Record Quarterly Revenue of $155m
text: Rocket Lab reported strong third-quarter performance with record quarterly revenue of $155m and a GAAP gross margin hitting 37%. The aerospace company achieved a 48% year-over-year revenue increase, demonstrating significant business momentum. During Q3, the company secured 17 Electron launch contracts and remains positioned to exceed 20 launches for the full year. The organization completed its acquisition of Geost, an electro-optical and infrared sensor manufacturer, for up to $325 million, expanding its national security capabilities across launch, spacecraft, and payload systems. Rocket Lab also achieved important infrastructure milestones, officially opening Launch Complex 3 for its Neutron medium-lift reusable rocket. The company updated its timeline, expecting Neutron to arrive at the launch facility in Q1 2026, pending qualification testing. For Q4 guidance, management projects revenue between $170–180 million and adjusted EBITDA losses of $23–29 million. The company exited the quarter with over $1 billion in liquidity following its at-the-market offering program, strengthening its financial position for strategic investments and M&A opportunities.
